purpose: Test the full routing recipe: (1) right-sized answer set, (2) reflex-defeated answer, (3) non-decorative route, (4) domain rotation, (5) plain-word answers. Self-assess whether the difficulty knob actually moved vs the (easy) Olympics stress test. Facts web-verified. Candidate batch.

candidates.q: Which college football program has won more games than any other in history — the only one ever to top 1,000 wins?
candidates.reflex_defeater: yes — beats the Alabama (recency) / Notre Dame (lore) reflex
candidates.note: 1,011 wins, first to 1,000; Ohio State 2nd at 975. The '1,000' clue is a fair boundary, not a giveaway of which school.

candidates.q: Which U.S. state is home to more top-division (FBS) college football teams than any other?
candidates.reflex_defeater: weak — Texas is a plausible first guess
candidates.note: 13 FBS teams; Ohio 2nd with 8. A real prune (TX vs FL vs CA vs OH) but TX is guessable.

candidates.q: Which bowl game has been played longer than any other, decades ahead of the rest?
candidates.reflex_defeater: weak — 'granddaddy of them all' is known
candidates.note: First played 1902. Its 'oldest bowl' reputation makes it recall-ish.

candidates.q: Players from which school have won the Heisman Trophy more times than from any other?
candidates.reflex_defeater: yes — beats the Notre Dame / Ohio State guess
candidates.note: 8 winners (Bush's reinstated 2024); Ohio State, Oklahoma, Notre Dame tied at 7. The near-tie adds real doubt.

candidates.q: In which decade was the very first college football game played, between two New Jersey schools?
candidates.route: NON-decorative — solver must know the first game (1869) to place the decade; the year is not given
candidates.note: Rutgers 6, Princeton 4, in 1869. Reason: post-Civil War, 19th century → 1860s-70s. Routes the subject onto the decade set without handing over the year.

candidates.q: How many teams now make the College Football Playoff?
candidates.note: Expanded from 4 to 12 in 2024. Topical recall for fans.

difficulty_audit: Skews medium, materially harder than the Olympics set. The three hard ones (1 Michigan, 4 USC, 5 the 1869 decade) earn it the right way: 1 and 4 via reflex-defeated answers (subject knowledge required), 5 via a non-decorative route (derive the year). The three easier ones (2 Texas, 3 Rose Bowl, 6 CFP-12) have correct-but-canonical answers, so the field doesn't get pruned — the knob is OFF on those. Conclusion: the recipe's difficulty knob is the reflex-defeater + non-decorative route; where I engaged it, difficulty moved; where I took the canonical fact, it stayed easy.
